implementation: navigationController.navigationBar.prefersLargeTitles = true; viewController.navigationItem.largeTitleDisplayMode = .always; // or .automatic for scroll-collapse behavior
anti_pattern: Fixed small navigation titles for all screens
notes: Large titles are a defining HIG navigation pattern for top-level hierarchy screens

implementation: let tabBar = UITabBarController(); tabBar.viewControllers = [homeNav feedNav searchNav profileNav]; tabBar.selectedIndex = 0; tabBar.tabBar.tintColor = .systemBlue;
anti_pattern: Custom tab bar built from UIView or UIStackView
notes: UITabBarController provides system-integrated tab behavior with correct animations and state

implementation: let tableView = UITableView(frame: .zero style: .insetGrouped); tableView.backgroundColor = .systemGroupedBackground; // Cells automatically get rounded corners and inset margins
anti_pattern: UITableView plain style for settings and grouped lists
notes: insetGrouped matches iOS Settings and System UI style with built-in corner rounding

implementation: let itemSize = NSCollectionLayoutSize(widthDimension: .fractionalWidth(0.5) heightDimension: .estimated(200)); let item = NSCollectionLayoutItem(layoutSize: itemSize); let groupSize = NSCollectionLayoutSize(widthDimension: .fractionalWidth(1.0) heightDimension: .estimated(200)); let group = NSCollectionLayoutGroup.horizontal(layoutSize: groupSize subitems: [item]); let section = NSCollectionLayoutSection(group: group); section.interGroupSpacing = 16; section.contentInsets = NSDirectionalEdgeInsets(top: 16 leading: 16 bottom: 16 trailing: 16);
anti_pattern: UICollectionViewFlowLayout for complex multi-section layouts
notes: Compositional layout handles self-sizing cells and complex grid/shelf patterns declaratively

implementation: var config = UIButton.Configuration.filled(); config.title = 'Continue'; config.cornerStyle = .large; config.buttonSize = .large; let button = UIButton(configuration: config primaryAction: UIAction { _ in }); button.tintColor = .systemBlue;
anti_pattern: Legacy setTitle/setBackgroundColor with manual styling
notes: UIButton.Configuration (iOS 15+) handles all visual states including highlighted and disabled cleanly

implementation: let vc = DetailViewController(); if let sheet = vc.sheetPresentationController { sheet.detents = [.medium() .large()]; sheet.prefersGrabberVisible = true; sheet.prefersScrollingExpandsWhenScrolledToEdge = true; } present(vc animated: true)
anti_pattern: Full-screen modals for content that warrants a sheet
notes: Half-sheet detents match iOS system UI patterns for contextual detail views

pattern: UIAlertController for alerts and action sheets
implementation: // Alert: let alert = UIAlertController(title: 'Delete?' message: 'This cannot be undone.' preferredStyle: .alert); alert.addAction(UIAlertAction(title: 'Delete' style: .destructive { _ in })); alert.addAction(UIAlertAction(title: 'Cancel' style: .cancel)); present(alert animated: true); // Action Sheet on iPad: alert.popoverPresentationController?.barButtonItem = barButton
anti_pattern: Custom modal dialogs built from UIView
notes: System alerts and action sheets follow HIG exactly and are VoiceOver-accessible out of the box

implementation: let searchController = UISearchController(searchResultsController: resultsVC); searchController.searchResultsUpdater = self; searchController.obscuresBackgroundDuringPresentation = false; navigationItem.searchController = searchController; navigationItem.hidesSearchBarWhenScrolling = true;
anti_pattern: UITextField manually placed in navigation bar for search
notes: UISearchController integrates with large title navigation and provides correct iOS search UX

implementation: let image = UIImage(systemName: 'heart.fill'); let imageView = UIImageView(image: image); imageView.preferredSymbolConfiguration = UIImage.SymbolConfiguration(pointSize: 24 weight: .medium); imageView.tintColor = .systemRed;
anti_pattern: Custom PNG icons for system actions already covered by SF Symbols
notes: SF Symbols scale with Dynamic Type and adapt to light/dark mode automatically

implementation: label.font = UIFont.preferredFont(forTextStyle: .headline); label.adjustsFontForContentSizeCategory = true; // For custom fonts: label.font = UIFontMetrics(forTextStyle: .body).scaledFont(for: customFont); label.adjustsFontForContentSizeCategory = true;
anti_pattern: Hardcoded font sizes that ignore user's Accessibility text size setting
notes: Required for accessibility compliance; large text users need scalable fonts

implementation: view.backgroundColor = .systemBackground; titleLabel.textColor = .label; subtitleLabel.textColor = .secondaryLabel; separatorView.backgroundColor = .separator; // Tinted surfaces: card.backgroundColor = .secondarySystemGroupedBackground;
anti_pattern: Hardcoded UIColor(red:green:blue:) values without dark mode variants
notes: Semantic colors adapt automatically to dark mode and high contrast without any extra code

pattern: UIView.animate with spring damping and velocity
implementation: UIView.animate(withDuration: 0.5 delay: 0 usingSpringWithDamping: 0.7 initialSpringVelocity: 0.8 options: .curveEaseOut animations: { view.transform = .identity view.alpha = 1 } completion: nil); // iOS 17+: UIView.animate(springDuration: 0.5) { view.transform = .identity }
anti_pattern: UIView.animate with linear or ease-in-out for interactive element transitions
notes: Spring animation matches UIKit's default feel for cards sheets and presented view controllers

implementation: let generator = UIImpactFeedbackGenerator(style: .medium); generator.prepare(); generator.impactOccurred(); // Styles: .light .medium .heavy .rigid .soft // For selections: UISelectionFeedbackGenerator // For notifications: UINotificationFeedbackGenerator
anti_pattern: No haptic feedback on destructive or confirmation interactions
notes: Haptics are a core Apple interaction primitive; missing them makes apps feel unpolished

implementation: NSLayoutConstraint.activate([ label.topAnchor.constraint(equalTo: view.layoutMarginsGuide.topAnchor constant: 16) label.leadingAnchor.constraint(equalTo: view.layoutMarginsGuide.leadingAnchor) label.trailingAnchor.constraint(equalTo: view.layoutMarginsGuide.trailingAnchor) ]); // Use safeAreaLayoutGuide for full-bleed containers
anti_pattern: Hardcoded frame values or magic numbers for positioning
notes: layoutMarginsGuide adapts to device size class and safe area automatically

implementation: let blurEffect = UIBlurEffect(style: .systemMaterial); let effectView = UIVisualEffectView(effect: blurEffect); effectView.frame = containerView.bounds; effectView.autoresizingMask = [.flexibleWidth .flexibleHeight]; containerView.addSubview(effectView); // Add content to effectView.contentView
anti_pattern: Simulating blur with semi-transparent views
notes: UIVisualEffectView uses Metal compositing for true real-time blur matching system materials

implementation: let interaction = UIContextMenuInteraction(delegate: self); view.addInteraction(interaction); // delegate method: func contextMenuInteraction(_ interaction: UIContextMenuInteraction configurationForMenuAtLocation location: CGPoint) -> UIContextMenuConfiguration? { return UIContextMenuConfiguration(actionProvider: { _ in UIMenu(children: [ UIAction(title: 'Share' image: UIImage(systemName: 'square.and.arrow.up')) { _ in }, UIAction(title: 'Delete' image: UIImage(systemName: 'trash') attributes: .destructive) { _ in } ]) }) }
anti_pattern: Long-press gesture recognizer with custom popover menu
notes: UIMenu renders system-standard context menus with correct animations haptics and accessibility

implementation: func tableView(_ tableView: UITableView trailingSwipeActionsConfigurationForRowAt indexPath: IndexPath) -> UISwipeActionsConfiguration? { let delete = UIContextualAction(style: .destructive title: 'Delete') { _ _ completion in self.deleteItem(at: indexPath); completion(true); }; delete.image = UIImage(systemName: 'trash'); return UISwipeActionsConfiguration(actions: [delete]); }
anti_pattern: Custom pan gesture recognizers for swipe-to-delete
notes: System swipe actions get correct animation haptics and accessibility labeling automatically

implementation: let insets = NSDirectionalEdgeInsets(top: 16 leading: 16 bottom: 16 trailing: 16); section.contentInsets = insets; // Standard padding: 16pt (compact) 20pt (regular) // Card padding: 16pt all sides // List row: 16pt horizontal 11pt vertical
anti_pattern: Hardcoded or inconsistent padding values across different views
notes: 16pt horizontal and 16pt vertical insets match the iOS standard reading margin

implementation: view.layer.cornerRadius = 16; view.layer.cornerCurve = .continuous; view.clipsToBounds = true; // For card-style cells view.layer.masksToBounds = true;
anti_pattern: Standard CALayer cornerRadius without .continuous curve
notes: Continuous corner curve (superellipse) matches iPhone hardware corners and Apple UI system-wide

implementation: override func traitCollectionDidChange(_ previousTraitCollection: UITraitCollection?) { super.traitCollectionDidChange(previousTraitCollection); if traitCollection.hasDifferentColorAppearance(comparedTo: previousTraitCollection) { updateColors(); } } func updateColors() { view.backgroundColor = UIColor.systemBackground; // Use only semantic colors - they update automatically }
anti_pattern: Manually overriding interface style or ignoring trait collection changes
notes: traitCollectionDidChange is the correct hook for custom color logic; semantic colors need no hook
